prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep in the cerulean sea, where coral castles spiraled and schools of neon fish zipped by like underwater fireworks, lived a tiny octopus named Inky. Inky wasn’t like the other boisterous sea creatures who loved to play hide-and-seek among the anemones or sing silly songs with the dolphins. Inky was a very, very shy octopus. His eight arms, usually so good at wiggling and exploring, would often knot themselves together when he saw a new face, and his ink sac, meant for quick escapes, often released just a tiny, nervous puff of black when someone swam too close. More than anything, though, Inky wished he had a friend to share his favorite pearly shell collection with.
